revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{日本の古墳 |名称= 唐櫃山古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府藤井寺市国府 |位置= |形状= 前方後円墳 |規模=径53ｍ |築造年...'
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 唐櫃山古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府藤井寺市国府 |位置= |形状= 前方後円墳 |規模=径53ｍ |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石槨（室） |被葬者= |出土品=甲冑 馬具 ガラス玉 |史跡指定= |特記事項=墳丘の大部分消滅 }} '''唐櫃山古墳'''（からとやまこふん）とは、[[大阪府]] [[藤井寺市]]国府に所在した[[前方後円墳]]。市ノ山古墳の周濠外にあったが1955年に道路工事により消滅した。緊急の発掘調査により、[[石棺]]や[[遺物]]が出土している。[[古市古墳群]]に属する古墳である。
== 概要== 墳丘長53ｍ、後円部径38ｍ、前方部幅21ｍを測る[[前方後円墳]]であった。[[府道堺大和高田線]]の敷設工事によって、後円部を含む墳丘の大半が破壊された。それに先立ち北野耕平らが発掘調査を実施した。後円部に主軸と平行した[[竪穴式石室| 竪穴式石槨]]が造られており、内部に家形石棺を内蔵していた。石棺はすぐ近くにあった[[長持山古墳]]の２つある石棺の1つと形態が酷似しており、石材も阿蘇溶結凝灰岩で共通するものであった。<ref>天野「基礎的研究」(2008)p72</ref>。 ==出土遺物と時期 == 内部はすでに盗掘を受けていたが、棺内からは多数のガラス小玉や丸玉が、棺外からは金銅製三輪玉、鹿角刀装具、刀剣、鉄鏃，2領の3角板鋲留[[短甲]]、[[馬具]]などが出土した。道路拡幅に伴う大阪府教育委員会による1981年2000年の発掘調査では後円部の裾部や周濠の一部とともに、[[円筒埴輪]]、朝顔形埴輪、家型埴輪、鶏形埴輪が出土している。付近にある[[市ノ山古墳]]と同時期の5世紀中葉と考えられる<ref>一瀬「基礎的研究」(2008)p.196</ref>。
==参考文献 == *天野末喜「唐櫃山古墳」『近畿地方における大型古墳群の基礎的研究』 科学研究「近畿地方における大型古墳群の基礎的研究」（研究代表 白石太一郎）研究グループ発行所 株式会社 六一書房2008年 72項
*一瀬和夫「唐櫃山」 同上書 196項
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}
==関連項目 == * [[古市古墳群]] * [[長持山古墳]]
==外部リンク == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html] 石棺と修羅 藤井寺市教育委員会文化財保護課
* [[category:古墳]] * [[category:前方後円墳]] * [[category:大阪府の古墳]] * [[category:藤井寺市]] * [[category:河内国]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 唐櫃山古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府藤井寺市国府 |位置= |形状= 前方後円墳 |規模=径53ｍ |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石槨（室） |被葬者= |出土品=甲冑 馬具 ガラス玉 |史跡指定= |特記事項=墳丘の大部分消滅 }} '''唐櫃山古墳'''（からとやまこふん）とは、[[大阪府]] [[藤井寺市]]国府に所在した[[前方後円墳]]。市ノ山古墳の周濠外にあったが[[1955年]]（[[昭和]]30年）に道路工事により消滅した。緊急の発掘調査により、[[石棺]]や[[遺物]]が出土している。[[古市古墳群]]に属する古墳である。
== 概要 == 墳丘長53ｍ、後円部径38ｍ、前方部幅21ｍを測る[[前方後円墳]]であった。[[府道堺大和高田線]]の敷設工事によって、後円部を含む墳丘の大半が破壊された。それに先立ち北野耕平らが発掘調査を実施した。後円部に主軸と平行した[[竪穴式石室| 竪穴式石槨]]が造られており、内部に家形石棺を内蔵していた。石棺はすぐ近くにあった[[長持山古墳]]の2つある石棺の1つと形態が酷似しており、石材も阿蘇溶結凝灰岩で共通するものであった。<ref>天野「基礎的研究」(2008)p72</ref>。
== 出土遺物と時期 == 内部はすでに盗掘を受けていたが、棺内からは多数のガラス小玉や丸玉が、棺外からは金銅製三輪玉、鹿角刀装具、刀剣、鉄鏃，2領の3角板鋲留[[短甲]]、[[馬具]]などが出土した。道路拡幅に伴う大阪府教育委員会による[[1981年]]、[[2000年]]の発掘調査では後円部の裾部や周濠の一部とともに、[[円筒埴輪]]、朝顔形埴輪、家型埴輪、鶏形埴輪が出土している。付近にある[[市ノ山古墳]]と同時期の5世紀中葉と考えられる<ref>一瀬「基礎的研究」(2008)p.196</ref>。

{{デフォルトソート:からとやまこふん}} [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:藤井寺市]] [[Category:河内国]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 唐櫃山古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府藤井寺市国府 |位置= |形状= 前方後円墳 |規模=全長53ｍ |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石槨（室） |被葬者= |出土品=甲冑 馬具 ガラス玉 |史跡指定= |特記事項=墳丘の大部分消滅 }} '''唐櫃山古墳'''（からとやまこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]国府に所在した[[前方後円墳]]。市ノ山古墳の周濠外にあったが[[1955年]]（[[昭和]]30年）に道路工事により墳丘の過半が消滅した（墳丘の北側の1部が個人の居宅の庭園として残存している）。緊急の発掘調査により、[[石棺]]や[[遺物]]が出土している。[[古市古墳群]]に属する古墳である。
== 概要 == 墳丘長53ｍ、後円部径38ｍ、前方部幅21ｍを測る[[前方後円墳]]であった。[[大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線|府道堺大和高田線]]の敷設工事によって、後円部を含む墳丘の大半が破壊された。それに先立ち北野耕平らが発掘調査を実施した。後円部に主軸と平行した[[竪穴式石室| 竪穴式石槨]]が造られており、内部に家形石棺を内蔵していた。石棺はすぐ近くにあった[[長持山古墳]]の2つある石棺の1つと形態が酷似しており、石材も阿蘇溶結凝灰岩で共通するものであった。<ref>天野「基礎的研究」(2008)p72</ref>。
=== 出土遺物と時期 === 内部はすでに盗掘を受けていたが、棺内からは多数のガラス小玉や丸玉が、棺外からは金銅製三輪玉、鹿角刀装具、刀剣、鉄鏃，2領の3角板鋲留[[短甲]]、[[馬具]]などが出土した。道路拡幅に伴う大阪府教育委員会による[[1981年]]、[[2000年]]の発掘調査では後円部の裾部や周濠の一部とともに、[[円筒埴輪]]、朝顔形埴輪、家型埴輪、鶏形埴輪が出土しており[[市ノ山古墳]]と同時期の5世紀中葉と考えられている<ref>一瀬「基礎的研究」(2008)p.196</ref>。 また2009年にも同教育委員会により発掘調査されており、笠形埴輪と盾形埴輪、円筒埴輪などの破片が出土している。埴輪は土師質のものと須恵質のものがあり須恵質のものが目立つ。円筒埴輪は市ノ山古墳外堤のものと共通する特徴を持ち、共通の埴輪窯から供給されていた可能性が指摘されている。周濠部分から出土した葺石はその石材から7割が付近を流れる石川の川原、残りを[[大和川]]と原川の川原などから採石されたと推定された<ref>西川「唐櫃山古墳」(2011)pp.14-34</ref>。
== 参考文献 == * 天野末喜「唐櫃山古墳」『近畿地方における大型古墳群の基礎的研究』 科学研究「近畿地方における大型古墳群の基礎的研究」（研究代表 白石太一郎）研究グループ発行所 株式会社六一書房 2008年 72項 * 一瀬和夫「唐櫃山」 同上書 196項 *西川寿勝『唐櫃山古墳』大阪府教育委員会 2011年 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 唐櫃山古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府藤井寺市国府 |位置= |形状= 前方後円墳 |規模=全長53ｍ |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石槨（室） |被葬者= |出土品=甲冑 馬具 ガラス玉 |史跡指定= |特記事項=墳丘の大部分消滅 }} '''唐櫃山古墳'''（からとやまこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]国府に所在した[[前方後円墳]]。市ノ山古墳の周濠外にあったが[[1955年]]（[[昭和]]30年）に道路工事により墳丘の過半が消滅した（墳丘の北側の1部が個人の居宅の庭園として残存している）。緊急の発掘調査により、[[石棺]]や[[遺物]]が出土している。[[古市古墳群]]に属する古墳である。
== 概要 == 墳丘長53ｍ、後円部径38ｍ、前方部幅21ｍを測る[[前方後円墳]]であった。[[大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線|府道堺大和高田線]]の敷設工事によって、後円部を含む墳丘の大半が破壊された。それに先立ち北野耕平らが発掘調査を実施した。後円部に主軸と平行した[[竪穴式石室| 竪穴式石槨]]が造られており、内部に家形石棺を内蔵していた。石棺はすぐ近くにあった[[長持山古墳]]の2つある石棺の1つと形態が酷似しており、石材も阿蘇溶結凝灰岩で共通するものであった。<ref>天野「基礎的研究」(2008)p72</ref>。
=== 出土遺物と時期 === 内部はすでに盗掘を受けていたが、棺内からは多数のガラス小玉や丸玉が、棺外からは金銅製三輪玉、鹿角刀装具、刀剣、鉄鏃，2領の3角板鋲留[[短甲]]、[[馬具]]などが出土した。道路拡幅に伴う大阪府教育委員会による[[1981年]]、[[2000年]]の発掘調査では後円部の裾部や周濠の一部とともに、[[円筒埴輪]]、朝顔形埴輪、家型埴輪、鶏形埴輪が出土しており[[市ノ山古墳]]と同時期の5世紀中葉と考えられている<ref>一瀬「基礎的研究」(2008)p.196</ref>。 また2009年にも同教育委員会により発掘調査されており、笠形埴輪と盾形埴輪、円筒埴輪などの破片が出土している。埴輪は土師質のものと須恵質のものがあり須恵質のものが目立つ。円筒埴輪は市ノ山古墳外堤のものと共通する特徴を持ち、共通の埴輪窯から供給されていた可能性が指摘されている。周濠部分から出土した葺石はその石材から7割が付近を流れる石川の川原、残りを[[大和川]]と原川の川原などから採石されたと推定された<ref>西川「唐櫃山古墳」(2011)pp.14-34</ref>。
== 参考文献 == * 天野末喜「唐櫃山古墳」『近畿地方における大型古墳群の基礎的研究』 科学研究「近畿地方における大型古墳群の基礎的研究」（研究代表 白石太一郎）研究グループ発行所 株式会社六一書房 2008年 72項 * 一瀬和夫「唐櫃山」 同上書 196項 *西川寿勝『唐櫃山古墳』大阪府教育委員会 2011年 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[古市古墳群]] * [[長持山古墳]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html 石棺と修羅 - 藤井寺市] （藤井寺市教育委員会文化財保護課）
{{デフォルトソート:からとやまこふん}} [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国]]

== 参考文献 == * 天野末喜「唐櫃山古墳」『近畿地方における大型古墳群の基礎的研究』 科学研究「近畿地方における大型古墳群の基礎的研究」（研究代表 白石太一郎）研究グループ発行所 株式会社六一書房 2008年 72項 * 一瀬和夫「唐櫃山」 同上書 196項 *西川寿勝『唐櫃山古墳』大阪府教育委員会 2011年 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[古市古墳群]] * [[長持山古墳]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html 石棺と修羅 - 藤井寺市] （藤井寺市教育委員会文化財保護課）

{{デフォルトソート:からとやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 唐櫃山古墳 |画像=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地= [[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]国府 |位置= |形状= [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模=墳丘長53ｍ　 |築造時期= [[5世紀]]代 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石槨 |被葬者= |出土品=甲冑・馬具・ガラス玉 |史跡=なし |特記事項=墳丘の大部分は非現存 }} '''唐櫃山古墳'''（からとやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]国府にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 市ノ山古墳の周濠外に位置し、[[1955年]]（[[昭和]]30年）に道路工事により墳丘の過半が消滅した（墳丘の北側の一部が個人の居宅の庭園として残存している）。緊急の発掘調査により、[[石棺]]や[[遺物]]が出土している。 == 概要 == 墳丘長53ｍ、後円部径38ｍ、前方部幅21ｍを測る[[帆立貝形古墳]]である。[[大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線|府道堺大和高田線]]の敷設工事によって、後円部を含む墳丘の大半が破壊された。それに先立ち北野耕平らが発掘調査を実施した。後円部に主軸と平行した[[竪穴式石室| 竪穴式石槨]]が造られており、内部に[[家形石棺]]を内蔵していた。石棺はすぐ近くにあった[[長持山古墳]]の2つある石棺の1つと形態が酷似しており、石材も阿蘇溶結凝灰岩で共通するものであった。天野「基礎的研究」(2008)p72。 == 出土遺物と時期 == 内部はすでに盗掘を受けていたが、棺内からは多数のガラス小玉や丸玉が、棺外からは金銅製三輪玉、鹿角刀装具、刀剣、鉄鏃，2領の3角板鋲留[[短甲]]、[[馬具]]などが出土した。道路拡幅に伴う大阪府教育委員会による[[1981年]]、[[2000年]]の発掘調査では後円部の裾部や周濠の一部とともに、[[円筒埴輪]]、朝顔形埴輪、家型埴輪、鶏形埴輪が出土しており[[市ノ山古墳]]と同時期の5世紀中葉と考えられている一瀬「基礎的研究」(2008)p.196。 また2009年にも同教育委員会により発掘調査されており、笠形埴輪と盾形埴輪、円筒埴輪などの破片が出土している。埴輪は土師質のものと須恵質のものがあり須恵質のものが目立つ。円筒埴輪は市ノ山古墳外堤のものと共通する特徴を持ち、共通の埴輪窯から供給されていた可能性が指摘されている。周濠部分から出土した葺石はその石材から7割が付近を流れる石川の川原、残りを[[大和川]]と原川の川原などから採石されたと推定された西川「唐櫃山古墳」(2011)pp.14-34。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 天野末喜「唐櫃山古墳」『近畿地方における大型古墳群の基礎的研究』 科学研究「近畿地方における大型古墳群の基礎的研究」（研究代表　白石太一郎）研究グループ発行所 株式会社六一書房 2008年 72項 * 一瀬和夫「唐櫃山」 同上書 196項 *西川寿勝『唐櫃山古墳』大阪府教育委員会　2011年 == 関連項目 == * [[古市古墳群]] * [[長持山古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html 石棺と修羅 - 藤井寺市]　（藤井寺市教育委員会文化財保護課） {{デフォルトソート:からとやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]]

revision.comment: 石棺の文化財指定について。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 唐櫃山古墳 |画像=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地= [[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]国府 |位置= |形状= [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模=墳丘長53ｍ　 |築造時期= [[5世紀]]代 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石槨 |被葬者= |出土品=甲冑・馬具・ガラス玉 |史跡=国の史跡「唐櫃山古墳」
（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち） |有形文化財=石棺（藤井寺市指定文化財） |特記事項=墳丘の大部分は非現存 }} '''唐櫃山古墳'''（からとやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]国府にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち）、石棺は藤井寺市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 市ノ山古墳の周濠外に位置し、[[1955年]]（[[昭和]]30年）に道路工事により墳丘の過半が消滅した（墳丘の北側の一部が個人の居宅の庭園として残存している）。緊急の発掘調査により、[[石棺]]や[[遺物]]が出土している。 == 概要 == 墳丘長53ｍ、後円部径38ｍ、前方部幅21ｍを測る[[帆立貝形古墳]]である。[[大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線|府道堺大和高田線]]の敷設工事によって、後円部を含む墳丘の大半が破壊された。それに先立ち北野耕平らが発掘調査を実施した。後円部に主軸と平行した[[竪穴式石室| 竪穴式石槨]]が造られており、内部に[[家形石棺]]を内蔵していた。石棺はすぐ近くにあった[[長持山古墳]]の2つある石棺の1つと形態が酷似しており、石材も阿蘇溶結凝灰岩で共通するものであった。天野「基礎的研究」(2008)p72。 == 出土遺物と時期 == 内部はすでに盗掘を受けていたが、棺内からは多数のガラス小玉や丸玉が、棺外からは金銅製三輪玉、鹿角刀装具、刀剣、鉄鏃，2領の3角板鋲留[[短甲]]、[[馬具]]などが出土した。道路拡幅に伴う大阪府教育委員会による[[1981年]]、[[2000年]]の発掘調査では後円部の裾部や周濠の一部とともに、[[円筒埴輪]]、朝顔形埴輪、家型埴輪、鶏形埴輪が出土しており[[市ノ山古墳]]と同時期の5世紀中葉と考えられている一瀬「基礎的研究」(2008)p.196。 また2009年にも同教育委員会により発掘調査されており、笠形埴輪と盾形埴輪、円筒埴輪などの破片が出土している。埴輪は土師質のものと須恵質のものがあり須恵質のものが目立つ。円筒埴輪は市ノ山古墳外堤のものと共通する特徴を持ち、共通の埴輪窯から供給されていた可能性が指摘されている。周濠部分から出土した葺石はその石材から7割が付近を流れる石川の川原、残りを[[大和川]]と原川の川原などから採石されたと推定された西川「唐櫃山古墳」(2011)pp.14-34。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 唐櫃山古墳（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち） - 2015年（平成27年）3月10日、既指定の史跡「古市古墳群」に追加指定平成27年3月10日文部科学省告示第43号。 === 藤井寺市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 唐櫃山古墳石棺 - 2022年（令和4年）3月24日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 天野末喜「唐櫃山古墳」『近畿地方における大型古墳群の基礎的研究』 科学研究「近畿地方における大型古墳群の基礎的研究」（研究代表　白石太一郎）研究グループ発行所 株式会社六一書房 2008年 72頁 * 一瀬和夫「唐櫃山」 同上書 196頁 *西川寿勝『唐櫃山古墳』大阪府教育委員会　2011年 == 関連項目 == * [[古市古墳群]] * [[長持山古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html 石棺と修羅 - 藤井寺市]　（藤井寺市教育委員会文化財保護課） {{デフォルトソート:からとやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 唐櫃山古墳 |画像=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地= [[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]国府 |位置= |形状= [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模=墳丘長53ｍ　 |築造時期= [[5世紀]]代 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石槨 |被葬者= |出土品=甲冑・馬具・ガラス玉 |史跡=国の史跡「唐櫃山古墳」
（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち） |有形文化財=石棺（藤井寺市指定文化財） |特記事項=墳丘の大部分は非現存 }} '''唐櫃山古墳'''（からとやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]国府にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち）、石棺は藤井寺市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 市ノ山古墳の周濠外に位置し、[[1955年]]（[[昭和]]30年）に道路工事により墳丘の過半が消滅した（墳丘の北側の一部が個人の居宅の庭園として残存している）。緊急の発掘調査により、[[石棺]]や[[遺物]]が出土している。 == 概要 == 墳丘長53ｍ、後円部径38ｍ、前方部幅21ｍを測る[[帆立貝形古墳]]である。[[大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線|府道堺大和高田線]]の敷設工事によって、後円部を含む墳丘の大半が破壊された。それに先立ち北野耕平らが発掘調査を実施した。後円部に主軸と平行した[[竪穴式石室| 竪穴式石槨]]が造られており、内部に[[家形石棺]]を内蔵していた。石棺はすぐ近くにあった[[長持山古墳]]の2つある石棺の1つと形態が酷似しており、石材も阿蘇溶結凝灰岩で共通するものであった。天野「基礎的研究」(2008)p72。 == 出土遺物と時期 == 内部はすでに盗掘を受けていたが、棺内からは多数のガラス小玉や丸玉が、棺外からは金銅製三輪玉、鹿角刀装具、刀剣、鉄鏃，2領の3角板鋲留[[短甲]]、[[馬具]]などが出土した。道路拡幅に伴う大阪府教育委員会による[[1981年]]、[[2000年]]の発掘調査では後円部の裾部や周濠の一部とともに、[[円筒埴輪]]、朝顔形埴輪、家型埴輪、鶏形埴輪が出土しており[[市ノ山古墳]]と同時期の5世紀中葉と考えられている一瀬「基礎的研究」(2008)p.196。 また2009年にも同教育委員会により発掘調査されており、笠形埴輪と盾形埴輪、円筒埴輪などの破片が出土している。埴輪は土師質のものと須恵質のものがあり須恵質のものが目立つ。円筒埴輪は市ノ山古墳外堤のものと共通する特徴を持ち、共通の埴輪窯から供給されていた可能性が指摘されている。周濠部分から出土した葺石はその石材から7割が付近を流れる石川の川原、残りを[[大和川]]と原川の川原などから採石されたと推定された西川「唐櫃山古墳」(2011)pp.14-34。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 唐櫃山古墳（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち） - 2015年（平成27年）3月10日、既指定の史跡「古市古墳群」に追加指定平成27年3月10日文部科学省告示第43号。 === 藤井寺市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 唐櫃山古墳石棺（考古資料） - 2022年（令和4年）3月24日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 天野末喜「唐櫃山古墳」『近畿地方における大型古墳群の基礎的研究』 科学研究「近畿地方における大型古墳群の基礎的研究」（研究代表　白石太一郎）研究グループ発行所 株式会社六一書房 2008年 72頁 * 一瀬和夫「唐櫃山」 同上書 196頁 *西川寿勝『唐櫃山古墳』大阪府教育委員会　2011年 == 関連項目 == * [[古市古墳群]] * [[長持山古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html 石棺と修羅 - 藤井寺市]　（藤井寺市教育委員会文化財保護課） {{デフォルトソート:からとやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=Karatoyama Kofun |画像=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |所属={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Added to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." {{日本の古墳 |名称=Karatoyama Kofun |画像=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |所属={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Added to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] == See Also == * [[House-shaped stone coffins]] * {{ill|Karabetsuyama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳}} - {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture ... * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] * {{ill|Karakitsu-yama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳}}

Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Added to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. {{日本の古墳 |名称=Karatoyama Kofun |画像=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |所属={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Added to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Added to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Added to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Added to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Added to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Karatoyama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:01-17]][[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] == See Also == * [[House-shaped stone coffins]] * {{ill|Karabetsuyama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳}} - {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture ... * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Karatoyama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] * {{ill|Karakitsu-yama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳}}

Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Added to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:dd]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Karatoyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] == See Also == * [[House-shaped stone coffins]] * {{ill|Karabetsuyama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳}} - {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture ... * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Karatoyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:kofun stubs]] * {{ill|Karakitsu-yama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Added to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Added to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Karatoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] == See Also == * [[House-shaped stone coffins]] * {{ill|Karabetsuyama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳}} - {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture ... * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Karatoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kofun stubs]] * {{ill|Karakitsu-yama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳}}

"Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Added to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Karatoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] == See Also == * [[House-shaped stone coffins]] * {{ill|Karabetsuyama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳}} - {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture ... * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Karatoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kofun stubs]] * {{ill|Karakitsu-yama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Added to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Karatoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] == See Also == * [[House-shaped stone coffins]] * {{ill|Karabetsuyama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳}} - {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture ... * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Karatoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kofun stubs]] * {{ill|Karakitsu-yama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳}}

Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Added to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Added to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Karatoyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Karatoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] == See Also == * [[House-shaped stone coffins]] * {{ill|Karabetsuyama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳}} - {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture ... * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Karatoyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Karatoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kofun stubs]] * {{ill|Karakitsu-yama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Added to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Karatoyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Karatoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] == See Also == * [[House-shaped stone coffins]] * {{ill|Karabetsuyama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳}} - {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture ... * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Karatoyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Karatoyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kofun stubs]] * {{ill|Karakitsu-yama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳}}
