{{Infobox ancient site |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Added to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Aqqed to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Aqqed to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Aqqed to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Aqqed to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Aqqed to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Aqqed to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Aqqed to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - Aqqed to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - A11ed to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - A11ed to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - A11ed to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - A11ed to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - A11ed to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - A11ed to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - A11ed to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |出土品=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - A11ed to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Kokufu |位置= |形状={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |規模=Mound length: 53m |築造時期=[[5th century]] |埋葬施設={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} |被葬者= |burial goods=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |有形文化財=Stone coffin (Fujidera City-designated cultural property) |特記事項=Most of the mound no longer exists }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun forming the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The stone coffin from this site is designated as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}} and artifacts. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == Artifacts and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. Artifacts found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. Artifacts recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - A11ed to the existing designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Stone coffin from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection
