But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}} |location={{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}} |位置= |type={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area=Mound length: 53m |built=[[:en:5th century|5th century]] |Burial Facility={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式石室}} |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]) |有形文化財=Sarcophagus (Fujidera City-Historic Site1 cultural property) |Special Notes=Most of the mound no longer exists |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is one of the kofun typeing the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], a Historic Site1 [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The Sarcophagus from this site is Historic Site1 as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]]. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|Sarcophagus|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} and burial goods. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|1=Osaka Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 12|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441406}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式石室}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochiyama Kofun|qq=}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == burial goods and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. burial goods found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. burial goods recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]].Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - A11ed to the existing Historic Site of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Sarcophagus from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochiyama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) == See Also == * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped Sarcophaguss]] * {{ill|Karabetsuyama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳|1=Karatoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karabetsuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatoyama Kofun|qq=}} - [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture ... * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Karakitsu-yama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳|1=Karatoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakitsu-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatoyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|唐櫃山古墳|version=99262944|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11418587}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - A11ed to the existing Historic Site of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Sarcophagus from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochiyama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) == See Also == * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped Sarcophaguss]] * {{ill|Karabetsuyama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳|1=Karatoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karabetsuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatoyama Kofun|qq=}} - {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture ... * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Karakitsu-yama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳|1=Karatoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakitsu-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatoyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|唐櫃山古墳|version=99262944|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11418587}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.comment: adding dummy category to make categories work
revision.text:
[[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] [[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] [[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] [[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}} |location={{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}} |位置= |type={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area=Mound length: 53m |built={{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} |Burial Facility={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式石室}} |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group}}) |有形文化財=Sarcophagus (Fujidera City-Historic Site1 cultural property) |Special Notes=Most of the mound no longer exists |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is one of the kofun typeing the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group}}, a Historic Site1 [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The Sarcophagus from this site is Historic Site1 as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|WD=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|Sarcophagus|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} and burial goods. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|1=Osaka Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 12|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441406}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式石室}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochiyama Kofun|qq=}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == burial goods and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. burial goods found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. burial goods recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|WD=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - A11ed to the existing Historic Site of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Sarcophagus from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochiyama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) == See Also == * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped Sarcophaguss]] * {{ill|Karabetsuyama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳|1=Karatoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karabetsuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatoyama Kofun|qq=}} - {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture ... * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Karakitsu-yama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳|1=Karatoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakitsu-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatoyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|唐櫃山古墳|version=99262944|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11418587}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - A11ed to the existing Historic Site of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Sarcophagus from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochiyama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) == See Also == * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped Sarcophaguss]] * {{ill|Karabetsuyama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳|1=Karatoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karabetsuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatoyama Kofun|qq=}} - {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera|1=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture ... * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Karakitsu-yama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳|1=Karatoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakitsu-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatoyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|唐櫃山古墳|version=99262944|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11418587}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] [[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] [[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] [[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} |location={{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|1=Fujiidera}} |位置= |type={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area=Mound length: 53m |built={{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} |Burial Facility={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式石室}} |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}) |有形文化財=Sarcophagus (Fujidera City-Historic Site1 cultural property) |Special Notes=Most of the mound no longer exists |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is one of the kofun typeing the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, a Historic Site1 [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The Sarcophagus from this site is Historic Site1 as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|WD=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun|1=Ichinoyama Kofun}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|Sarcophagus|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} and burial goods. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|1=Osaka Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 12|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441406}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式石室}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochiyama Kofun|qq=}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == burial goods and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. burial goods found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. burial goods recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|WD=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun|1=Ichinoyama Kofun}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - A11ed to the existing Historic Site of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Sarcophagus from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochiyama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) == See Also == * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped Sarcophaguss]] * {{ill|Karabetsuyama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳|1=Karatoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karabetsuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatoyama Kofun|qq=}} - {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera|1=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture ... * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Karakitsu-yama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳|1=Karatoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakitsu-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatoyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|唐櫃山古墳|version=99262944|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11418587}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式石室}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochiyama Kofun|qq=}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == burial goods and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. burial goods found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. burial goods recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|qid=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun|1=Ichinoyama Kofun}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - A11ed to the existing Historic Site of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Sarcophagus from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochiyama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) == See Also == * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped Sarcophaguss]] * {{ill|Karabetsuyama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳|1=Karatoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karabetsuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatoyama Kofun|qq=}} - {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera|1=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture ... * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Karakitsu-yama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳|1=Karatoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakitsu-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatoyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|唐櫃山古墳|version=99262944|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11418587}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] [[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] [[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] [[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Karatoyama Kofun |image=[[File:Karatoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} |location={{ill|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|1=Fujiidera}} |位置= |type={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area=Mound length: 53m |built={{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} |Burial Facility={{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式石室}} |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Armor, horse gear, glass beads |史跡=National Historic Site "Karatoyama Kofun" (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}) |有形文化財=Sarcophagus (Fujidera City-Historic Site1 cultural property) |Special Notes=Most of the mound no longer exists |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Karatoyama Kofun''' (唐櫃山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Kokufu, [[Fujidera City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is one of the kofun typeing the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, a Historic Site1 [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. The Sarcophagus from this site is Historic Site1 as a Fujidera City tangible cultural property. The kofun was situated outside the moat of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|qid=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun|1=Ichinoyama Kofun}}. In 1955, road construction destroyed most of the mound, leaving only the northern portion as part of a private garden. Emergency excavations yielded a {{ill|Sarcophagus|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} and burial goods. == Overview == The mound measures 53 meters in length, with a rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 21 meters wide at the front section. The mound was largely destroyed during the construction of the {{ill|Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|ja|大阪府道・奈良県道12号堺大和高田線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture Route 12 and Nara Prefecture Route 12|1=Osaka Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 12|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11441406}}. Prior to its destruction, archaeologist Kohei Kitano conducted an excavation. A {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式石室}} was identified, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis in the rear circular section, containing a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. The coffin was remarkably similar to one of the coffins from {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochiyama Kofun|qq=}} and made from the same Aso welded tuff.Amano "Basic Research" (2008), p. 72. == burial goods and Dating == Although the burial chamber had been looted, numerous small glass beads and round beads were recovered from inside the coffin. burial goods found outside the coffin included a gilt bronze tri-wheel bead, deer antler sword fittings, swords, iron arrowheads, two sets of triangular iron plate armor, and horse gear. Excavations conducted by the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education in 1981 and 2000, related to road widening projects, uncovered parts of the rear section and moat. burial goods recovered included {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and chicken-shaped haniwa, dating the kofun to the mid-5th century, contemporary with {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|qid=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun|1=Ichinoyama Kofun}}.Ichinose "Basic Research" (2008), p. 196. A 2009 excavation also uncovered fragments of umbrella-shaped haniwa, shield-shaped haniwa, and cylindrical haniwa. These haniwa were made from both Haji and Sue-type clays, with Sue-type haniwa being more prevalent. The cylindrical haniwa shared characteristics with those from the outer embankment of Ichinoyama Kofun, suggesting they came from the same kiln.Nishikawa "Karatoyama Kofun" (2011), pp. 14-34. Fukiishi (covering stones) recovered from the moat were found to consist of approximately 70% stones from the nearby Ishikawa River, with the remainder sourced from the Yamato River and Haragawa River. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Karatoyama Kofun (part of the "Furuichi Kofun Group") - A11ed to the existing Historic Site of the "Furuichi Kofun Group" on March 10, 2015.March 10, 2015: MEXT Notification No. 43. === Fujidera City-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Sarcophagus from Karatoyama Kofun (archaeological material) - Designated on March 24, 2022. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Amano, Suemitsu. "Karatoyama Kofun." *Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region*. Research Group of "Basic Research on Large Kofun Groups in the Kinki Region" (Principal Investigator: Taichiro Shiraishi), Rokuichi Shobo, 2008, p. 72. * Ichinose, Kazuo. "Karatoyama." Same publication, p. 196. * Nishikawa, Toshikatsu. *Karatoyama Kofun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2011. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Nagamochiyama Kofun|ja|長持山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochiyama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1067,97,143.html Stone Coffins and Sledges - Fujidera City] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) == See Also == * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped Sarcophaguss]] * {{ill|Karabetsuyama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳|1=Karatoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karabetsuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatoyama Kofun|qq=}} - {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera|1=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture ... * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Karakitsu-yama Kofun|ja|唐櫃山古墳|1=Karatoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakitsu-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatoyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|唐櫃山古墳|version=99262944|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:唐櫃山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11418587}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
